abstract:
Abstract
                  
                    This article systematically reviews the literature on online task crowdwork to investigate the complex relationship between technology and work design on crowdwork platforms. We highlight the diverse interpretations and uses of technology, specifically platform features and algorithms, in relation to work design. Our review reveals that platform features serve as antecedents to work design characteristics, while algorithms are so intertwined with job execution that a new work characteristic is needed to model this interplay. We introduce this new work characteristic as
                    algorithmic embeddedness
                    and show that it varies in degree. When high, algorithmic embeddedness can be perceived as either an affordance or a constraint; when low, it has a limited impact on crowdworkers’ jobs. Our ‘gig characteristics model’ expands previous work design theories and offers a framework for understanding the design of contemporary jobs that rely highly on algorithms. To refine our model and better understand crowdwork dynamics, we provide an agenda for future research directions.

affiliation: University of Modena and Reggio Emilia
authors_full.name: Francesca Bellesia

authors_full.name: Fabiola Bertolotti

ob.ai_reason: 算法嵌入与零工工作设计，核心AI议题
ob.tldr: 系统综述在线任务众包文献，发现平台特征是工作设计的前因，算法与任务执行深度耦合，提出‘算法嵌入度’新工作特征，构建‘零工特征模型’，扩展工作设计理论，为算法化岗位提供框架。

title: Algorithmic Embeddedness and the ‘Gig’ Characteristics Model: Examining the Interplay between Technology and Work Design in Crowdwork
title_zh: 算法嵌入与零工特征模型：众包工作中技术与工作设计的互动
